page.title: Lagoon 380 catamaran: Best Boat in Marbella for Sailing-day catamaran with kids

data.meta_description: Is the Lagoon 380 catamaran best for your Marbella family day? 10 pax, 11.6m, departs Puerto Banús with skipper, drinks & snacks included.
data.summary: The Lagoon 380 catamaran is the best choice in Marbella for a sailing day with kids, offering stability, space for 10 guests, and all-inclusive amenities from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Length: 11.6m – stable catamaran design perfect for families
Capacity: 10 guests – ample space for kids to move safely
Departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella's luxury marina
All-inclusive: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks & IVA
Shallow draft allows close coastal cruising and beach stops
Sailing catamaran – quiet, eco-friendly, and gentle motion
Pricing on request via WhatsApp – tailored for your group

faq.q: Is the Lagoon 380 catamaran best for a family with young children?
faq.a: Yes, the Lagoon 380 is widely considered the best catamaran in Marbella for families with young children. Its wide beam and low centre of gravity provide exceptional stability, reducing seasickness. The forward trampoline and spacious cockpit give kids room to play safely under supervision.

faq.q: How many people can the Lagoon 380 accommodate?
faq.a: The Lagoon 380 comfortably holds up to 10 guests, including children. This capacity makes it ideal for family reunions or small group outings. The catamaran layout includes a large saloon, two hull cabins, and plenty of deck space.

faq.q: What is included in the Lagoon 380 charter price?
faq.a: Every Lagoon 380 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras – just show up and enjoy.

faq.q: Where does the Lagoon 380 depart from?
faq.a: The Lagoon 380 departs from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet your skipper at the dock, board the catamaran, and set sail along the Costa del Sol towards Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or Estepona.

faq.q: Can we stop for swimming and snorkelling?
faq.a: Absolutely. The shallow draft of the Lagoon 380 allows it to anchor close to beaches and coves. Popular stops include Cala Cortés and the waters off Cabopino. Snorkelling gear is usually available on request.

faq.q: Is the Lagoon 380 suitable for a half-day charter?
faq.a: Yes, the Lagoon 380 is ideal for half-day trips (4 hours) or full-day charters (8 hours). Pricing is available on request via WhatsApp, so you can customise the duration to fit your family's schedule.

faq.q: How does the Lagoon 380 compare to motor yachts for kids?
faq.a: The Lagoon 380's sailing catamaran design offers a quieter, smoother ride than most motor yachts. Without the engine drone and with less rolling motion, children often feel more comfortable. The trampoline netting is also a unique fun feature.

faq.q: Do we need sailing experience to charter the Lagoon 380?
faq.a: No experience is needed. Your charter includes a professional skipper who handles all navigation and sailing. You can relax, help raise a sail if you wish, or simply enjoy the ride along the Marbella coastline.

data.body_html:
If you are searching for a Lagoon 380 catamaran best for a family day on the water in Marbella, look no further. This 11.6-metre sailing catamaran accommodates up to 10 guests and departs from Puerto Banús with everything included: skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. Its stable twin-hull design makes it the top choice for parents wanting a safe, comfortable, and fun day out with kids along the Costa del Sol.

Why the Lagoon 380 is the best catamaran in Marbella for families

Unlike monohull sailboats or fast motor yachts, the Lagoon 380 offers exceptional stability thanks to its wide beam and low centre of gravity. Children can move freely between the cockpit, saloon, and forward trampoline without the risk of heavy heeling. The gentle sailing motion reduces the chance of seasickness, and the shallow draft allows you to anchor close to sandy beaches such as Cala del Faro or Cabopino. For a relaxed, all-inclusive family charter, this catamaran is hard to beat.

Lagoon 380 specifications and capacity

  • Length: 11.6 metres
  • Capacity: 10 guests (including children)
  • Departure point: Puerto Banús, Marbella
  • Type: Sailing catamaran
  • Skipper: Licensed and included
  • Inclusions: Fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%)

All charters are all-inclusive. You do not pay extra for the skipper, fuel, or basic refreshments. The price is quoted via WhatsApp based on your preferred duration and date.

What to expect on board the Lagoon 380

Your day begins at Puerto Banús, where you meet the skipper and board the catamaran. The spacious cockpit has a dining table and cushioned seating, perfect for lunch while anchored. The forward trampoline is a favourite spot for kids to lie down and watch the sea. Inside, the saloon offers shade and a galley. The two hulls contain cabins and a bathroom, though most families spend the day on deck. The skipper will sail along the coast, stop for swimming, and point out landmarks like La Concha mountain and the Golden Mile.

Best itineraries for a family catamaran day

The Lagoon 380 can explore several kid-friendly destinations from Puerto Banús:

  • Cabo Pino: 30 minutes sail east – calm waters and a sandy beach
  • Cala del Faro: 20 minutes west – a quiet cove for snorkelling
  • Estepona: 1 hour west – charming old town and marina
  • Marbella Marina: 15 minutes east – stop for a walk along the promenade

The skipper can tailor the route to your family's preferences, ensuring plenty of swimming stops and relaxation time.

Pricing and how to book

Pricing for the Lagoon 380 is available on request via WhatsApp. Unlike our Tier A motorboats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39), which have fixed rates from €749 for 2 hours, the Lagoon 380 is quoted individually to match your group size and desired itinerary. Contact us on WhatsApp for a personalised quote. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during July and August.

What sets the Lagoon 380 apart from other boats in our fleet

Our fleet includes powerful motor yachts like the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94, but the Lagoon 380 offers something unique: a genuine sailing experience without the motion that bothers some guests. It is the only catamaran in our Puerto Banús fleet, making it the default choice for families who prioritise space and stability over speed. If you want to teach children the basics of sailing in a safe environment, this is the boat.

Safety and comfort for children

The Lagoon 380 is equipped with lifelines, guardrails, and a non-slip deck. The trampoline netting is strong and safe for supervised play. Life jackets for children are available on request. The skipper holds all required licences and safety certifications. Because the catamaran sails rather than motors at high speed, the noise level is low, and the ride is smooth even in light chop.

Frequently asked questions

Is the Lagoon 380 catamaran best for a family with young children?

Yes, the Lagoon 380 is widely considered the best catamaran in Marbella for families with young children. Its wide beam and low centre of gravity provide exceptional stability, reducing seasickness. The forward trampoline and spacious cockpit give kids room to play safely under supervision.

How many people can the Lagoon 380 accommodate?

The Lagoon 380 comfortably holds up to 10 guests, including children. This capacity makes it ideal for family reunions or small group outings. The catamaran layout includes a large saloon, two hull cabins, and plenty of deck space.

What is included in the Lagoon 380 charter price?

Every Lagoon 380 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras – just show up and enjoy.

Where does the Lagoon 380 depart from?

The Lagoon 380 departs from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. You'll meet your skipper at the dock, board the catamaran, and set sail along the Costa del Sol towards Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or Estepona.

Can we stop for swimming and snorkelling?

Absolutely. The shallow draft of the Lagoon 380 allows it to anchor close to beaches and coves. Popular stops include Cala Cortés and the waters off Cabopino. Snorkelling gear is usually available on request.

Is the Lagoon 380 suitable for a half-day charter?

Yes, the Lagoon 380 is ideal for half-day trips (4 hours) or full-day charters (8 hours). Pricing is available on request via WhatsApp, so you can customise the duration to fit your family's schedule.

How does the Lagoon 380 compare to motor yachts for kids?

The Lagoon 380's sailing catamaran design offers a quieter, smoother ride than most motor yachts. Without the engine drone and with less rolling motion, children often feel more comfortable. The trampoline netting is also a unique fun feature.

Do we need sailing experience to charter the Lagoon 380?

No experience is needed. Your charter includes a professional skipper who handles all navigation and sailing. You can relax, help raise a sail if you wish, or simply enjoy the ride along the Marbella coastline.

Ready to book the Lagoon 380 catamaran best for your family day in Marbella? Browse all our Marbella boat charters or contact us on WhatsApp for a personalised quote. We look forward to welcoming you aboard.
